_source_note: ECE R23 II-Year (2-1 and 2-2) subject data sourced from Tirumala Engineering College's published JNTUK R23 ECE syllabus PDF: https://www.tecnrt.org/R23_FINIAL_SYLLABUS/SECOND%20YEAR/ECE%20II-I%20%26%20II-II%20SYLLABUS.pdf (downloaded 2026-07-01, 20 pages, extracted with pdf-parse). Tirumala Engineering College (Narasaraopet) is a JNTUK-affiliated, NON-autonomous college that follows the university curriculum directly (no local adaptation), so this reflects the JNTUK-wide R23 ECE baseline. The university's own forum PDF (forum.universityupdates.in) is login-gated and could not be fetched; this affiliated-college copy was used instead. Every record's source.origin_url points at the actual file used. verified = subject name, L-T-P-C credits AND full unit topics confirmed by reading the document; needs_verification = name/credits confirmed but full unit detail not transcribed in this pass (see each record's notes).

subjects.name: Signals and Systems

units.title: Introduction
topics: Definition and classification of signals and systems
Operations on signals: time-shifting, time-scaling, amplitude-shifting/scaling
Complex exponential and sinusoidal signals; singularity functions (impulse, step, signum, ramp)
Analogy between vectors and signals; orthogonal signal space; mean square error; orthogonality in complex functions

units.title: Fourier Series and Fourier Transform
topics: Fourier series of continuous-time periodic signals; Dirichlet's conditions
Trigonometric and exponential Fourier series; complex Fourier spectrum
Fourier transform of standard and periodic signals; properties
Introduction to Hilbert transform

units.title: Analysis of Linear Systems
topics: Linear system, impulse response, LTI and LTV systems
Convolution in time and frequency domain; transfer function of an LTI system
Filter characteristics; distortionless transmission; signal and system bandwidth
Ideal LPF/HPF/BPF; Paley-Wiener criterion; bandwidth-rise time relation

units.title: Laplace Transforms
topics: Region of convergence (ROC) and constraints for various signal classes
Properties of Laplace transforms; inverse Laplace transform
Relation between Laplace transform and Fourier transform
Laplace transform via waveform synthesis

units.title: Sampling Theorem and Z-Transforms
topics: Sampling theorem for band-limited signals; impulse, natural and flat-top sampling
Reconstruction; aliasing; band-pass sampling
Z-transform of a discrete sequence; ROC; inverse Z-transform; properties
Distinction between Laplace, Fourier and Z transforms

seo.title: Signals and Systems - JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1 Signals and Systems syllabus: unit-wise topics (Fourier, Laplace, Z-transforms, sampling) and credits.

subjects.notes: Name, 3-0-0-3 credits, and full 5-unit syllabus confirmed by reading the Tirumala Engineering College JNTUK R23 ECE PDF (non-autonomous, follows university curriculum).

subjects.name: Electronic Devices and Circuits

units.title: Review of Semiconductor Physics and Junction Diode Characteristics
topics: Hall effect, continuity equation, law of junction, Fermi-Dirac function, Fermi level
PN junction energy band diagram; open-circuited and biased p-n junction
Diode equation, V-I characteristics, temperature dependence
Diode resistance and diode capacitance

units.title: Special Semiconductor Devices and Diode Circuits
topics: Zener diode and breakdown mechanisms; Varactor, LED, Photodiode, Tunnel diode, UJT, SCR
Diode as a circuit element; load-line concept; clippers and clampers; peak detector; comparators
Rectifiers: half-wave, full-wave, bridge; derivations
Filters: inductor, capacitor, pi-section; ripple factor comparison

units.title: Transistor Characteristics, Biasing and Thermal Stabilization
topics: BJT current components; CB, CE, CC configurations; Ebers-Moll model; phototransistor
Need for biasing; operating point; load-line analysis
Biasing methods: fixed bias, collector-to-base, self bias; stability factors (S, S', S'')
Bias compensation; thermal runaway and thermal stability

units.title: Small Signal Low Frequency Transistor Amplifier Models (BJT)
topics: Two-port network; transistor hybrid model; determination and conversion of h-parameters
Generalized analysis of transistor amplifier using h-parameters
Analysis of CB, CE, CC amplifiers (exact and approximate)
Comparison of transistor amplifiers

units.title: FET and MOSFET
topics: FET types; JFET operation, characteristics, biasing; small-signal model
MOSFET structure and operation in triode and saturation regions
V-I characteristics of MOSFET; MOS transconductance; small-signal model
PMOS and NMOS transistors; comparison of bipolar and MOS devices

seo.title: Electronic Devices and Circuits - JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1 Electronic Devices and Circuits syllabus: diodes, rectifiers, BJT/FET characteristics, biasing and amplifier models.

subjects.notes: Name, 3-0-0-3 credits, and full 5-unit syllabus confirmed by reading the source PDF.

subjects.name: Switching Theory and Logic Design

units.title: Number Systems, Codes and Boolean Theorems
topics: Radix conversions; r's and (r-1)'s complements of signed numbers
Gray, BCD, Excess-3, 2421 codes; error detection and correction (parity, Hamming code)
Boolean theorems; De Morgan's theorems; complementation and duality
Logic operations; SOP and POS forms; NAND-NAND and NOR-NOR realizations

units.title: Minimization Techniques and Combinational Logic Design
topics: K-map (up to 6 variables) and Quine-McCluskey (tabular) method
Half/full adder and subtractor; 4-bit adder-subtractor
BCD adder, Excess-3 adder, carry look-ahead adder
Code converters using Karnaugh method

units.title: Combinational Logic using MSI/LSI and PLDs
topics: Encoder, decoder, multiplexer, de-multiplexer; higher-order from lower-order circuits
Boolean functions using decoders and multiplexers
Priority encoder, 4-bit digital comparator, seven-segment decoder
PLDs: PROM, PAL, PLA - structures and realization

units.title: Sequential Circuits I
topics: Synchronous vs asynchronous circuits; NAND/NOR latches
Flip-flops: RS, JK, T, D with reset/clear; flip-flop conversions
Ripple and synchronous counters; Johnson and ring counters
Registers: buffer, shift, bidirectional, universal shift register

units.title: Sequential Circuits II
topics: Finite state machine; state diagrams and state tables; reduction of state tables
Analysis of clocked sequential circuits
Mealy to Moore conversion and vice-versa
Sequence generator and sequence detector design (with/without overlapping)

seo.title: Switching Theory & Logic Design - JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1 Switching Theory and Logic Design syllabus: number systems, K-maps, combinational and sequential circuits.

subjects.notes: Name, 3-0-0-3 credits, and full 5-unit syllabus confirmed by reading the source PDF.

subjects.name: Environmental Science

units.title: Multidisciplinary Nature and Natural Resources
topics: Scope and importance of environmental studies; need for public awareness
Renewable and non-renewable resources
Forest, water, mineral, food and energy resources and associated problems

units.title: Ecosystems and Biodiversity
topics: Ecosystem structure and function; energy flow; ecological succession; food chains/webs/pyramids
Forest, grassland, desert and aquatic ecosystems
Biodiversity: genetic/species/ecosystem; value, hotspots, threats
Conservation of biodiversity: in-situ and ex-situ

units.title: Environmental Pollution and Solid Waste Management
topics: Air, water, soil, marine, noise, thermal pollution and nuclear hazards
Solid waste management: urban and industrial wastes
Disaster management: floods, earthquakes, cyclones, landslides

units.title: Social Issues and the Environment
topics: Sustainable development; water conservation and rainwater harvesting; watershed management
Environmental ethics; climate change, global warming, acid rain, ozone depletion
Environment protection acts and legislation

units.title: Human Population and the Environment
topics: Population growth and explosion; family welfare programmes
Environment and human health; human rights; value education
Role of information technology in environment and human health

seo.title: Environmental Science - JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1 Environmental Science (audit course) syllabus: natural resources, ecosystems, pollution and social issues.

subjects.notes: Audit course (non-credit): the source lists L-T-P as 2-0-0 with credits shown as '-' (no credit); recorded as C:0. Full 5-unit syllabus confirmed by reading the source PDF.

subjects.name: Electronic Devices and Circuits Lab

seo.title: Electronic Devices & Circuits Lab - JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1 Electronic Devices and Circuits Lab: experiments on diodes, rectifiers, BJT/FET characteristics, biasing and amplifiers.

subjects.notes: Experiment-based lab (clippers/clampers, rectifiers, BJT-CE and FET-CS characteristics, SCR/UJT, biasing, CE/CC/CS amplifiers). Name, 0-0-3-1.5 credits and experiment list confirmed from the source PDF; no unit-wise theory syllabus exists for a lab, so units left empty.

subjects.name: Signals and Systems Lab

seo.title: Signals and Systems Lab - JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1 Signals and Systems Lab: signal generation, operations, convolution/correlation, Fourier, Laplace and Z-transforms.

subjects.notes: Experiment-based lab (basic signal generation, operations on signals, energy/power, convolution and correlation, Fourier/Laplace/Z-transforms). Name, 0-0-3-1.5 credits and experiment list confirmed from the source PDF; units left empty (lab).

subjects.name: Data Structures using Python

seo.title: Data Structures using Python - JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1 Data Structures using Python: skill course with programming experiments (searching, sorting, stacks, queues, linked lists, BST).

subjects.notes: Skill Enhancement course delivered as programming experiments (classes/inheritance, comprehensions, linear/binary search, bubble/selection/merge/quick sort, stacks, queues, singly/doubly linked lists, BST). Name, 0-1-2-2 credits and experiment list confirmed from the source PDF; no unit-wise theory syllabus given, so units left empty.

units.title: Operations on Single and Multiple Random Variables - Expectations

seo.title: Probability Theory & Stochastic Process - R23 ECE 2-1
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-1 Probability Theory and Stochastic Process: random variables, random processes (temporal and spectral) and noise.

subjects.name: Managerial Economics and Financial Analysis

units.title: Managerial Economics
topics: Nature, meaning and significance of managerial economics
Demand concept and law of demand; demand elasticity (types and measurement)
Demand forecasting: factors and methods

units.title: Production and Cost Analysis
topics: Production function; least-cost combination; short-run and long-run production
Isoquants and isocosts; cost concepts and behaviour
Break-even analysis and determination of break-even point

units.title: Business Organizations and Markets
topics: Forms of business organizations: sole proprietary, partnership, joint stock, public sector
Market types: perfect and imperfect competition, monopoly, monopolistic, oligopoly
Price-output determination; pricing methods and strategies

units.title: Capital Budgeting
topics: Working capital types, components and sources; estimating requirements
Capital budgeting: features, proposals, methods and evaluation
Payback, ARR, NPV and IRR methods

units.title: Financial Accounting and Analysis
topics: Double-entry bookkeeping; journal, ledger, trial balance
Final accounts: trading account, profit and loss account, balance sheet
Ratio analysis: liquidity, activity, capital structure and profitability ratios

seo.title: Managerial Economics & Financial Analysis - R23 ECE 2-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-2 Managerial Economics and Financial Analysis: demand, production and cost, markets, capital budgeting and financial accounting.

subjects.notes: Name, 2-0-0-2 credits and full 5-unit syllabus confirmed by reading the source PDF.

subjects.name: Linear Control Systems

units.title: Introduction
topics: Open-loop and closed-loop control systems and their differences
Feedback characteristics and effects of feedback
Mathematical models; differential equations; impulse response and transfer functions
Translational and rotational mechanical systems

units.title: Transfer Function Representation and Time Response Analysis
topics: Transfer function of DC/AC servo motors and synchros
Block diagram algebra; signal flow graph and Mason's gain formula
Standard test signals; first- and second-order time response; time-domain specifications
Steady-state errors and error constants

units.title: Stability Analysis in S-Domain and Root Locus
topics: Concept of stability; Routh's stability criterion; conditional stability
Limitations of Routh's stability criterion
Root locus concept and construction; effect of adding poles and zeros

units.title: Frequency Response Analysis
topics: Correlation between time and frequency response
Polar plots and Bode plots
Nyquist stability criterion

units.title: Classical Control Design and State Space Analysis
topics: Lag, lead and lead-lag compensators; PID controllers
State, state variables and state model; state models from block diagrams
Diagonalization; solving state equations; state transition matrix
Controllability and observability

seo.title: Linear Control Systems - JNTUK R23 ECE 2-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-2 Linear Control Systems syllabus: transfer functions, time and frequency response, stability, root locus and state space.

subjects.notes: Name, 3-0-0-3 credits and full 5-unit syllabus confirmed by reading the source PDF.

subjects.name: Electromagnetic Waves and Transmission Lines

units.title: Electrostatics
topics: Coordinate systems; Coulomb's law; electric field intensity and flux density
Gauss law and applications; electric potential; Maxwell's two electrostatic equations; energy density
Poisson's and Laplace's equations
Capacitance: parallel plate and coaxial capacitors

units.title: Magnetostatics and Maxwell's Equations
topics: Biot-Savart law; Ampere's circuital law and applications; magnetic flux density
Magnetic scalar and vector potentials; forces; inductances and magnetic energy
Time-varying fields; Faraday's law; displacement current; Maxwell's equations
Boundary conditions

units.title: EM Wave Characteristics
topics: Wave equations for conducting and dielectric media; uniform plane waves
Propagation in lossy/lossless dielectrics, free space and good conductors; skin depth; polarization
Reflection and refraction (normal and oblique incidence)
Brewster angle, critical angle, total internal reflection; Poynting theorem

units.title: Transmission Lines - I
topics: Types, parameters, T and pi equivalent circuits; transmission line equations
Primary and secondary constants; characteristic impedance; propagation constant
Phase and group velocities; infinite, lossless and distortionless lines

units.title: Transmission Lines - II
topics: Input impedance, reflection coefficient, VSWR; shorted, open and matched lines
UHF lines as circuit elements
Smith chart construction and applications; quarter-wave transformer; single stub matching

seo.title: Electromagnetic Waves & Transmission Lines - R23 ECE 2-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-2 Electromagnetic Waves and Transmission Lines: electrostatics, magnetostatics, EM waves, transmission lines and Smith chart.

subjects.notes: Name, 3-0-0-3 credits and full 5-unit syllabus confirmed by reading the source PDF.

subjects.name: Electronic Circuit Analysis

units.title: Small Signal High Frequency Transistor Amplifier Models
topics: BJT at high frequencies; hybrid-pi common emitter model; conductances and capacitances
High-frequency parameters from low-frequency parameters; CE short-circuit current gain
Cut-off frequencies, frequency response and gain-bandwidth product
FET common-source and common-drain amplifiers at high frequencies

units.title: Multistage Amplifiers
topics: Classification of amplifiers; methods of coupling; cascaded amplifier analysis
Two-stage RC coupled amplifier
Darlington pair, cascode, bootstrap emitter follower
Differential amplifier using BJT

units.title: Feedback Amplifiers
topics: Feedback principle and concept; types of feedback; feedback topologies
Characteristics of negative feedback amplifiers
Generalized analysis and method of analysis; performance comparison

units.title: Oscillators
topics: Oscillator principle and condition for oscillations; types of oscillators
RC phase-shift and Wien bridge oscillators (BJT and FET)
LC oscillators: Hartley and Colpitts
Frequency and amplitude stability of oscillators

units.title: Power and Tuned Amplifiers
topics: Classes A to H; Class A, Class B push-pull, complementary symmetry, Class AB, Class C
Thermal stability and heat sinks
Tuned amplifiers: Q-factor, single/double/staggered tuned amplifiers

seo.title: Electronic Circuit Analysis - JNTUK R23 ECE 2-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-2 Electronic Circuit Analysis: high-frequency models, multistage amplifiers, feedback, oscillators, power and tuned amplifiers.

subjects.notes: Name, 3-0-0-3 credits and full 5-unit syllabus confirmed by reading the source PDF.

subjects.name: Analog Communications

units.title: Amplitude Modulation
topics: Need for modulation; frequency division multiplexing
AM time and frequency domain description; single-tone modulation; power relations
Generation of AM: square law and switching modulator
Detection of AM: square law detector and envelope detector

units.title: DSB and SSB Modulation
topics: DSB-SC generation (balanced and ring modulator) and detection (coherent, Costas, squaring loop)
SSB-SC generation (frequency and phase discrimination) and coherent demodulation
Vestigial sideband modulation and envelope detection
Comparison and applications of AM techniques

units.title: Angle Modulation
topics: Phase and frequency modulation; single-tone FM; spectrum; narrowband and wideband FM
Transmission bandwidth of FM
Generation of FM: direct and indirect methods
Detection of FM: balanced discriminator, zero-crossing detector, PLL; FM vs AM

units.title: Radio Transmitters and Receivers
topics: AM and FM transmitters; effect of feedback; frequency stability
Receiver types: TRF and superheterodyne; RF section, frequency changing and tracking
Intermediate frequency, AGC; FM receiver and amplitude limiting
Comparison of FM and AM receivers

units.title: Noise and Pulse Analog Modulation
topics: Noise sources and noise figure; noise in DSB/SSB/AM and angle modulation systems
Threshold effect; pre-emphasis and de-emphasis
Pulse analog modulation: PAM, PWM, PPM (generation and detection)
Time division multiplexing; TDM vs FDM

seo.title: Analog Communications - JNTUK R23 ECE 2-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-2 Analog Communications: amplitude modulation, DSB/SSB, angle modulation, radio transmitters/receivers and noise.

subjects.notes: Name, 3-0-0-3 credits and full 5-unit syllabus confirmed by reading the source PDF.

seo.title: Switching Theory & Logic Design Lab - R23 ECE 2-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-2 Switching Theory and Logic Design Lab: logic gates, combinational and sequential circuit design, counters and registers.

subjects.notes: Experiment-based lab (logic gate verification, combinational design, decoders/multiplexers, flip-flops, ring/Johnson counters, universal shift register, seven-segment display). Name, 0-0-3-1.5 credits and experiment list confirmed from the source PDF; units left empty (lab).

subjects.name: Electronic Circuit Analysis Lab

seo.title: Electronic Circuit Analysis Lab - JNTUK R23 ECE 2-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 ECE 2-2 Electronic Circuit Analysis Lab: feedback amplifiers, oscillators, multistage, power and tuned amplifiers (Multisim + hardware).

subjects.notes: Simulation (Multisim) + hardware lab (Ft determination, voltage-series/current-shunt feedback amplifiers, RC/Wien/Hartley/Colpitts oscillators, RC coupled and Darlington amplifiers, Class A/B power amplifiers, tuned amplifiers). Name, 0-0-3-1.5 credits and experiment list confirmed from the source PDF; units left empty (lab).
